config.title: Berlin Rooftop Bars - FAQ
config.subtitle: A local's answers on Berlin rooftop bars: which ones are worth it, what they cost, dress codes, the best time to go, and whether you need to book.

items.q: What are the best rooftop bars in Berlin?
items.a: My short list is five: Klunkerkranich in Neukölln for a cheap, plant-filled sunset roof; Monkey Bar in City West for a polished tenth-floor skyline cocktail; House of Weekend on Alexanderplatz for a central roof under the TV Tower that turns into a club; Deck 5 in Prenzlauer Berg for a beach bar with real sand on a car-park roof; and Solar near Anhalter Bahnhof for a dressed-up sky bar you reach by an exposed glass lift. The casual roofs are the more typically Berlin experience.

items.q: Are Berlin rooftop bars free to enter?
items.a: Mostly, yes. Monkey Bar and Solar are free to enter but charge proper cocktail prices. The casual roofs like Deck 5 are free with normal Berlin bar prices, while Klunkerkranich often has a small entry fee once the evening music starts. House of Weekend is free-ish as a daytime sun-deck but charges a club entry at night. Berlin is still a partly cash city, so carry some, even though most places take cards.

items.q: Is there a dress code at Berlin rooftop bars?
items.a: At the casual roofs, no. Klunkerkranich and Deck 5 are come-as-you-are, and shorts and trainers are completely normal. The exception is Solar, which enforces a dress code and turns people away in beachwear, shorts or flip-flops. Monkey Bar is smart-casual but relaxed. When in doubt, the hotel and sky bars expect you to look a little tidier than the car-park roofs.

items.q: When is the best time to go to a rooftop bar in Berlin?
items.a: Aim to arrive about 45 minutes before sunset. That lets you get a drink and a good spot while it is still light, then watch the sky turn gold and the city lights come on. Berlin sunset times move a lot across the year, from close to 21:30 in late June to soon after 18:00 in late October, so check tonight's actual sunset rather than assuming a fixed hour. Most roofs are open-air, so keep an eye on the weather too.

items.q: Do I need to book a rooftop bar in Berlin?
items.a: For the casual roofs, no, you just turn up, though there can be a short queue for the lift at busy times. For Monkey Bar and especially Solar, a reservation is a good idea on sunny evenings and weekends, when the best window seats and tables fill quickly. Deck 5 and Klunkerkranich are seasonal and weather-dependent, and House of Weekend often announces its summer daytime roof opening on social media at short notice, so check the venue's own channels on the day.

items.q: Which Berlin rooftop bar has the best view?
items.a: It depends what you want to see. For the classic TV Tower and central Berlin skyline, House of Weekend on Alexanderplatz puts it right in front of you. For a wide western sunset over the city, Klunkerkranich in Neukölln and Monkey Bar in City West are both excellent, with Monkey Bar also looking down into the Zoo. Solar gives the highest, most panoramic window view. For pure sunset mood on a budget, Klunkerkranich is the one I recommend most.
